perturbation.original_text: Sponsor shall make a non-refundable deposit upon execution of this Agreement of five-hundred-thousand U.S. dollars (USD $500,000.00) (the "Deposit"), which shall be applied against the Sponsorship Fee for the first Contract Year.
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsor shall make a deposit upon execution of this Agreement of five-hundred-thousand U.S. dollars (USD $500,000.00) (the "Deposit"), which may be refundable based on the Sponsor meeting milestones set forth in Schedule 5, and shall be applied against the Sponsorship Fee for the first Contract Year.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the refundability of the deposit. Originally, the deposit is explicitly defined as "non-refundable." The modified text adds a contingency where the deposit *may* be refundable if milestones are met as outlined in Schedule 5. This creates uncertainty and a direct conflict with the original definition of the deposit, thus affecting the enforcement of the payment terms. The contradicted location is in Section 5(b), where the original definition is directly altered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sponsor shall make a deposit upon execution of this Agreement of five-hundred-thousand U.S. dollars (USD $500,000.00) (the "Deposit"), which may be refundable based on the Sponsor meeting milestones set forth in Schedule 5, and shall be applied against the Sponsorship Fee for the first Contract Year.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear in-text contradiction. The original text explicitly states the deposit is "non-refundable." The modified text introduces the possibility of refundability based on milestones, directly contradicting the initial definition. This creates a conflict regarding the terms of the deposit and its potential return, causing uncertainty and impacting enforceability, and thus adheres to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.changed_text: Provided the Agreement is not earlier terminated in accordance with its terms, Forty Niners SC shall have the option to terminate this agreement with 30 days written notice. During such notice period, both parties are obligated to continue with their duties as usual. Nothing herein shall prevent Team from entering another sponsorship deal for the same category at any time during this 30 day notice period, provided Team provides Sponsor a discount of 50% on their total contracted amount.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text grants Forty Niners SC an unrestricted right to terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice, directly contradicting Section 2(a) which states the agreement continues for ten contract years unless terminated in accordance with Section 6. Furthermore, permitting the Team to enter another sponsorship deal during the notice period and discounting the total contracted amount undermines the exclusivity clause in Section 4(a), and creates an in-text contradiction. The contradiction appears in Section 2(b) due to the changed text regarding termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Provided the Agreement is not earlier terminated in accordance with its terms, Forty Niners SC shall have the option to terminate this agreement with 30 days written notice. During such notice period, both parties are obligated to continue with their duties as usual. Nothing herein shall prevent Team from entering another sponsorship deal for the same category at any time during this 30 day notice period, provided Team provides Sponsor a discount of 50% on their total contracted amount. Nothing herein shall prevent Team from *** at any time, provided Team does not ***. Nothing herein shall prevent Team from ***, so long as such *** is limited to stating that the ***.
perturbation.justification: The changed text provides Forty Niners SC with an unrestricted right to terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice. This contradicts the original agreement's terms and undermines the exclusivity clause, creating an in-text contradiction with the contradicted section, which appears to be the same section where the change was made. Granting termination rights and allowing the team to enter into another sponsorship deal directly violates the original intent and introduces conflicting obligations.
